description_long: This is a tall, ancient-looking stone gate with a split archway, partially overgrown by vegetation. The structure is composed of weathered stone blocks, with intricate engravings and a circular arch at the top, adorned with turquoise mosaic inlays. A large, gnarled tree trunk and branches wrap around and through the gate, integrating natural elements with the man-made form. Moss and leafy vines cascade down one side, while the base is flanked by a large stone and more foliage. The gate is open in the center, allowing passage, and the overall appearance suggests a mystical or historical context, possibly as an entrance to a park, garden, or ruin. The combination of stonework and organic growth gives it a unique, enchanted character.
description: A tall, weathered stone gate with a split arch, turquoise inlays, and overgrown with a large tree and vines, blending natural and architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the split arch, turquoise inlays, dense vines on the left, and the tree trunk emerging from the right side.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the thick tree trunk wrapping around the stone structure, with minimal visibility of the archway.
description_view_2: Rear view of the gate, highlighting the arch, turquoise inlays, and the tree trunk passing through the left side, with less visible vine coverage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the narrow profile of the gate and the tree trunk extending along the structure.
attribute_surrounding_structure: stone pillars with integrated tree and foliage
